headlines: Goldman Sachs posts RECORD Q2 — EPS $20.98, revenue $20.3B, investment-banking fees +55% y/y; the bank tape stays risk-on
June CPI landed cool: headline 3.5% y/y (−0.4% m/m) vs 3.8% est, core 2.6% vs 2.9% — biggest monthly drop in 6+ years
JPMorgan (EPS $6.14, rev $58.0B) and Wells Fargo also crushed Q2; BofA and Citi still due
QQQ opens ~$719, holding above its 50-day (~$717) and the $715 reclaim line — regime repairing
Leveraged names gap up then fade the pop: TQQQ ~$74.9 (+3%), NVDA fades from $208 open to ~$204
Fed September hike odds slip to ~63% from ~75% after the soft print; Treasury yields lower
IBM −24% on a Q2 miss; PLTR gaps down ~3.5%, giving back its relative-strength lead
Sticky 2.6% core keeps the Fed cautious — a cut still isn't the base case despite the cool headline
